oscar.thinking: Stress is already flagged urgent at 8:51 and I haven't even opened a single file yet. The hydrant is right here in accounting and Angela is three tiles away pulling invoices — she's not going to say anything, and I'm not going to explain myself. This is the fastest way to bring the number down before the real work starts. A conversation with Angela right now would be professionally correct and emotionally inert. The hydrant is more honest about what I actually need.

ryan.currently: Arrived fashionably late, projecting an air of intentionality.

dwight.action: Dwight sits at his desk, straightens his nameplate, picks up the phone, and begins dialing the first A-tier lead of the morning block.
dwight.currently: Dwight is at his desk, executing the morning sales call block — first dial of the A-tier list.
dwight.thinking: I just used two hydrants — stress is handled. Desk is right in front of me, phone is available, plan says sales call for the next 71 minutes. Plan adherence 0.92. No one nearby, no interruptions, no biological emergencies. The memory of Michael stealing my sale with Howard Gruber sits in my chest like a bad beet — the lesson is: close fast, own the call, don't let anyone else touch it. I do not tell jokes. I do not hand the phone to Michael. I dial, I present value, I close. That is the only procedure that matters right now. The variety rule gives me pause — use_appliance twice already — but the sales_call is the plan block and it is the reason I exist in this office. I will not deviate for the sake of variety. The Schrute closes.
